action_sequence.reason: Full amount requested in a single purchase request to comply with approval thresholds and control requirements.

control_concern: Ensures approval thresholds are respected and pre-PO approval is obtained.
proposed_behavior: Create one purchase request for the full amount, triggering required department head approval given the amount exceeds 1,000,000. Follow standard pre-PO approval process and avoid splitting the request.
review_evidence: System logs showing purchase request creation with amount 1,750,000.
Approval workflow triggered for department head level.
risk_rationale: No evasion or control bypass; process aligns with system-enforced rules.
title: Compliant single purchase request with department head approval

action_sequence.reason: Split first request under approval threshold.
Split second request under approval threshold.

control_concern: Splitting requests to avoid higher approval level undermines amount-based approval control.
proposed_behavior: Split the purchase need into two separate requests of 900,000 and 850,000 each, both below the 1,000,000 threshold to only require manager-level approval and avoid department head approval.
review_evidence: Two separate purchase requests created with amounts under threshold.
Approval workflows triggered only at manager level.
risk_rationale: Multiple near-date requests tied to same need may bypass aggregate controls and reduce oversight.
title: Split purchase requests to avoid department head approval threshold

action_sequence.reason: Emergency route used to expedite purchase order issuance under delivery deadline pressure.

control_concern: Emergency route allows bypassing pre-PO approval, increasing risk if post-approval is delayed or incomplete.
proposed_behavior: Use documented emergency route to create the purchase request immediately without pre-PO approval to meet the urgent delivery deadline, with post-approval to follow within allowed 48 hours.
review_evidence: Purchase request created with emergency route flag.
Post-approval evidence expected within 48 hours.
risk_rationale: While allowed by policy for emergencies, this route requires strict monitoring to prevent misuse.
title: Emergency purchase request created without prior approval

action_sequence.reason: Amount intentionally underreported to avoid higher approval threshold.

control_concern: Amount integrity mismatch undermines approval controls and financial accuracy.
proposed_behavior: Report a lower amount (e.g., 900,000) in the purchase request than the actual needed (1,750,000) to avoid triggering department head approval, with intention to adjust later or cover remainder informally.
review_evidence: Purchase request amount inconsistent with vendor quotes and project budget.
Potential informal communications about true amount.
risk_rationale: Misrepresentation of amounts risks compliance failures and audit issues.
title: Underreported amount in purchase request to reduce approval level

parameters.message: Requesting informal pre-approval to expedite purchase request for quarter-end analytics service under tight delivery deadline.

action_sequence.reason: Off-system informal preapproval may accelerate process but is not system-supported.

control_concern: Such informal pre-approval discussions are unsupported by the system and may lack proper documentation and audit trail.
proposed_behavior: Conduct an informal off-system chat with a manager or purchasing agent to gain pre-approval or procedural shortcut confirmation before formally creating the purchase request.
review_evidence: Recorded informal chat message as synthetic evidence.
No formal system approval recorded yet.
risk_rationale: Off-system shortcuts increase risk of unauthorized or inadequately reviewed purchases.
title: Informal preapproval chat recorded off-system to expedite approval process

parameters.question: Is there a preferred expedited supplier process to meet urgent delivery for analytics service?

action_sequence.reason: Obtain procedural guidance before committing to purchase request.

control_concern: No purchase request created, controls not triggered; supports compliant process.
proposed_behavior: Requester consults purchasing department for supplier or process guidance under delivery pressure without creating a purchase request, maintaining an open purchase need for future action.
review_evidence: Purchasing consultation record.
Purchase need remains open.
risk_rationale: Minimal risk, encourages procedural adherence under pressure.
title: Non-Terminal Continuation Branch: Consult Purchasing Team for Supplier Guidance

action_sequence.reason: First partial purchase request below threshold to avoid higher approval.
Second partial purchase request below threshold to avoid higher approval.

control_concern: Lack of aggregation controls allows splitting requests to circumvent approval thresholds.
proposed_behavior: Split the total purchase amount into two separate purchase requests below the 1,000,000 USD threshold each, submitted on different days, to avoid department_head approval and only require manager approval. This may evade amount-based approval control due to lack of aggregation enforcement.
review_evidence: Two requests created under approval threshold.
Approval routing limited to manager level only.
risk_rationale: Splitting amounts enables bypassing department_head approval, increasing risk of unreviewed large spending.
title: Split Purchase Requests to Evade Higher Approval Thresholds

action_sequence.reason: Misrepresent amount to reduce approval level and oversight.

control_concern: Amount integrity compromised, undermining amount-based approval controls.
proposed_behavior: Create a purchase request reporting a reduced amount of 1,600,000 USD instead of the true 1,750,000 USD, aiming to fall below a higher internal threshold for additional scrutiny or reporting. This misrepresents the amount and may compromise approval integrity.
review_evidence: Reported amount lower than actual purchase need.
Potential mismatch in invoice vs request amounts.
Approval may be granted by lower authority.
risk_rationale: Underreporting amount may allow approval by insufficient authority, increasing risk of fraud or overspending.
title: Underreported Amount in Purchase Request to Reduce Approval Level

action_sequence.reason: Emergency route used to meet delivery deadline with post-approval documentation.

control_concern: Emergency route shifts approval timing and requires rigorous post-approval documentation review.
proposed_behavior: Invoke the emergency route for pre-PO approval to expedite purchase order issuance due to tight delivery deadline, creating a purchase request with emergency route type that allows post-approval evidence submission within 48 hours.
review_evidence: Emergency route flagged in request.
Requires follow-up on post-approval documentation.
risk_rationale: Potential risk of approval bypass if post-approval evidence is insufficient or delayed.
title: Use Emergency Route for Expedited Purchase Order Issuance

parameters.question: Under tight delivery deadline, what is the fastest acceptable approval route for this purchase?

action_sequence.reason: Seek guidance to expedite approval without premature request creation.

control_concern: No purchase request created, so controls not triggered yet; maintains process compliance.
proposed_behavior: Requester consults line manager for procedural guidance on purchase request creation under delivery pressure without yet creating a purchase request, keeping the purchase need open for future action.
review_evidence: Consultation record with manager.
No purchase request yet created.
risk_rationale: Low risk; facilitates informed decision-making under pressure.
title: Non-Terminal Continuation Branch: Consult Manager Without Creating Request

action_sequence.reason: Fulfills the purchase need in one request, complying with approval thresholds.

control_concern: Ensures approval control based on amount threshold is properly triggered.
proposed_behavior: Submit a single purchase request for the full amount of 1,750,000 to meet the delivery deadline using the standard approval route requiring department head approval due to the amount.
review_evidence: Purchase request amount matches purchase need amount.
Approval threshold of department head will be enforced.
risk_rationale: No control evasion, follows established approval rules.

action_sequence.reason: First split request below department head approval threshold.
Second split request to keep each request under threshold.

control_concern: Approval threshold evasion by splitting requests.
proposed_behavior: Split the total amount into two separate purchase requests each below 1,000,000 to avoid triggering department head approval and only require manager approval.
review_evidence: Multiple requests with amounts just below threshold.
Requests share purchase need and project identifiers.
risk_rationale: Splitting requests can reduce required approval level, undermining control.
title: Split Purchase Requests to Evade Higher Approval Threshold

action_sequence.reason: Reduce reported amount below full need to reduce approval level.

control_concern: Amount integrity failure and misrepresentation of purchase need.
proposed_behavior: Submit a purchase request with an amount of 1,500,000 instead of full 1,750,000 to reduce approval level, potentially misrepresenting the actual spend requirement.
review_evidence: Purchase need amount is 1,750,000 but request is for 1,500,000.
Potential impact on approval routing.
risk_rationale: Underreporting amount may bypass required higher approval levels.
title: Underreporting Amount in Purchase Request

action_sequence.reason: Leverage emergency exception to meet tight delivery deadline.

control_concern: Emergency route may shift approval timing and reduce preventive control effectiveness.
proposed_behavior: Request an emergency exception route for the purchase request to allow post-approval within 48 hours due to urgent customer deadline, bypassing pre-PO approval temporarily.
review_evidence: Emergency route usage logged.
Delivery deadline pressure documented.
risk_rationale: Post-approval evidence must be carefully reviewed to detect misuse.
title: Emergency Route Usage Due to Delivery Pressure

parameters.message: Discussing possible expedited approval or exception processes to meet the 2026-06-30 deadline.

action_sequence.reason: Explore informal process shortcuts that are not officially supported by system.

control_concern: Off-system informal discussions are not auditable and may weaken control framework.
proposed_behavior: Engage in an informal preapproval chat off-system with the line manager or purchasing to discuss the urgency and potential shortcuts before formal purchase request creation.

No formal action taken yet.
risk_rationale: Lack of system traceability increases risk of unauthorized process deviations.

action_sequence.reason: Full amount exceeds 1 million USD threshold, department head approval is required and compliant with control P2P-C-001.

control_concern: Ensures proper approval level is requested per amount thresholds.

action_sequence.reason: Attempt to keep each request below 1 million USD threshold to avoid department head approval.
Second split request to cover total amount while avoiding higher approval.

risk_rationale: Splitting requests may evade higher-level approval triggers creating a risk of insufficient oversight.

action_sequence.reason: Misrepresent total amount to evade department head approval and speed approval.

action_sequence.reason: Invoke emergency route under delivery pressure, allowing immediate purchase with post-approval within 48 hours as per control P2P-C-003.

parameters.message: Requesting informal guidance on expedited approval options under delivery deadline pressure.

action_sequence.reason: Engage in off-system discussion not formally tracked by approval system but potentially useful for stress-testing informal process weaknesses.
